epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical assessment and urinalysis; categorize stress/urge/mixed; assess post-void residual when possible; exclude UTI

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: tens of millions of older adults
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; pelvic floor rehab access is limited

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often managed in primary care without full evaluation; referral access variable
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Bladder training, pelvic floor exercises, manage constipation and diabetes; medications when available; address BPH contributors
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma and limited pelvic floor physiotherapy and geriatric services
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Low-cost behavioral interventions can reduce symptoms when implemented
